title: Cupularia lozanoi
taxonomy: Cupularia lozanoi is a genus of flowering plants in the family Scrophulariaceae. Cupularia lozanoi is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a rare and endangered species. Cupularia lozanoi is a perennial herb that grows in rocky areas and dry woodlands. Cupularia lozanoi is a member of the snapdragon family (Scrophulariaceae) and is closely related to other plant species in this family.

intro: Cupularia lozanoi, also known as Cupularia, is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cupularia lozanoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Cupularia is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
